A proposal to waive property tax on homes below 500 sq ft in Pune has triggered a major debate, as the Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) may face a financial loss of nearly ₹268 crore if the demand is approved. The Congress has formally placed the proposal before the standing committee, arguing that small home owners deserve relief amid rising inflation and economic stress. Civic officials, however, have warned that such a move could severely impact the corporation’s revenue, which is used to fund essential services like roads, water supply and infrastructure projects. PMC records show there are over 4.43 lakh properties under 500 sq ft that currently contribute significantly to the civic budget. Officials also fear that the exemption could encourage buyers to opt for smaller flats just to avoid taxes, increasing pressure on basic amenities. The issue has now sparked wider public discussion on balancing tax relief with the city’s development needs. Do you think small flat owners should get property tax exemption in Pune? ⁉️How much influence do “paid influencers” actually have? ⁉️ INFLUENCERS move voters more than MEMBERS OF CONGRESS. A sitting member of Congress can give a speech on the House floor that reaches a few thousand people on C SPAN. Meanwhile a single episode from Tucker Carlson or Megyn Kelly will reach millions of Americans within hours. One viral clip from Candace Owens, Glenn Greenwald, or Steve Bannon will circulate across X, Rumble, YouTube, Telegram and podcasts before most members of Congress have even finished their press release. And the reach does not stop there. Alex Jones built an audience over decades that still commands millions of loyal listeners. Nick Fuentes speaks directly to an entire segment of younger online voters. The Hodge Twins reach millions of everyday Americans through humor and commentary that spreads far beyond traditional political circles. Compare that to Congress. Most Americans could not name their own representative without Googling it. Yet millions can immediately tell you what Tucker, Megyn, or Candace said yesterday. Even the most visible members of Congress or former members of congress like Marjorie Taylor Greene, Thomas Massie, and Rand Paul only achieve massive reach when their message breaks out through influencer networks online. That is the real media ecosystem of modern politics. Influencers do not just comment on politics anymore. They set the tone. They frame the narrative. They tell voters who is fighting and who is selling out. By the time a midterm voter walks into a ballot box, there is a good chance the opinions shaping their vote were influenced more by a podcast, livestream, or viral clip than by a floor speech from Washington. But in the digital age, political gravity has shifted. The people with the microphones, the platforms, and the audiences are shaping the battlefield long before Election Day. 🎙️🇺🇸 PLEASE TELL ME YOU UNDERSTAND JUST HOW DANGEROUS THE “Paid Influencer Mafia” IS!

Under Section 4 of the 25th Amendment to the US Constitution, if a president is unable to discharge their powers and duties (including due to mental incapacity), the vice president and a majority of the Cabinet can send a written declaration to Congress. The VP then immediately becomes Acting President. If the president contests it, Congress has 21 days to decide by a two-thirds vote in both houses. This has never been used for mental health but applies generally.

I've read H.R.22 (119th Congress), the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act. It requires documentary proof of U.S. citizenship for federal voter registration: REAL ID indicating citizenship, valid passport, military ID + birth record, gov photo ID showing U.S. birth, or standard photo ID (like a driver's license) paired with a certified birth certificate, hospital birth record, naturalization cert, etc. Standard driver's licenses alone don't qualify, as they don't prove citizenship—matching the OP's point.
